# preprocessing.curate_data.remove_duplicate_timestamps { #spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.curate_data.remove_duplicate_timestamps }

```python
preprocessing.curate_data.remove_duplicate_timestamps(
    df,
    time_col='Time (UTC)',
    agg='mean',
)
```

Resolve duplicate timestamps across all data columns.
Groups rows that share the same ``time_col`` value and collapses them
using the chosen aggregation.  All columns except ``time_col`` are
aggregated.  The resulting frame is sorted chronologically, re-indexed,
and returned.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                                   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Default        |
|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| df       | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)         | Input dataframe containing ``time_col`` and one or more data columns.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | _required_     |
| time_col | [str](`str`)                                           | Name of the column that holds timestamps.  Defaults to ``"Time (UTC)"``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | `'Time (UTC)'` |
| agg      | [str](`str`) \| [Callable](`collections.abc.Callable`) | Aggregation applied when collapsing duplicate rows.  Accepts any string recognised by :meth:`pandas.core.groupby.GroupBy.agg` (``"mean"``, ``"median"``, ``"min"``, ``"max"``, ``"sum"``, ``"std"``, ``"var"``, ``"first"``, ``"last"``) as well as ``"mode"`` (most frequent value per group) or any custom callable. Defaults to ``"mean"``. | `'mean'`       |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                                         |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pd.DataFrame: Deduplicated dataframe with unique ``time_col`` rows, |
|        | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | sorted ascending by timestamp.                                      |

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                   | Description                               |
|--------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
|        | [KeyError](`KeyError`) | If ``time_col`` is not present in ``df``. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Mean-aggregate two data columns with the default time column:

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.curate_data import remove_duplicate_timestamps
df = pd.DataFrame(
     {
         "Time (UTC)": [
             "2026-01-01 00:00:00",
             "2026-01-01 00:00:00",
             "2026-01-01 01:00:00",
         ],
         "Load A": [100.0, 120.0, 130.0],
         "Load B": [200.0, 220.0, 210.0],
     }
    )
out = remove_duplicate_timestamps(df)
print(f"len(out): {len(out)}")
print(f"Load A: {float(out.loc[0, 'Load A'])}")
print(f"Load B: {float(out.loc[0, 'Load B'])}")
```

Median aggregation on a custom time column:

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from spotforecast2_safe.preprocessing.curate_data import remove_duplicate_timestamps
df2 = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "ts": ["2026-01-01", "2026-01-01", "2026-01-02"],
        "value": [10.0, 30.0, 20.0],
    }
)
out2 = remove_duplicate_timestamps(
    df2, time_col="ts", agg="median"
)
print(f"Value: {float(out2.loc[0, 'value'])}")
```
